purpose: Preserve repeated user feedback about Wizard System, voices, LLM Council, follow-up formatting, wave receipts, and response behavior so Codex does not forget it within the same or future threads.

user_feedback_to_preserve.topic: Tiny feedback should be applied, not become the whole answer
user_feedback_to_preserve.content: When the user gives formatting or behavior feedback, correct the output and continue the prior task. Do not make the whole reply about the error unless explicitly asked for an audit.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Patch the visible output directly; keep error discussion short and subordinate.

user_feedback_to_preserve.topic: Existing system must not be replaced
user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Wizard System is an addition to the existing voice/lane/follow-up system, not a replacement. Do not collapse existing voices, lanes, All bundles, or grouped follow-up fields into a smaller generic menu.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Use RETAINED/ADDITION logic when changing format behavior; preserve the existing structure unless explicitly told to remove it.

user_feedback_to_preserve.topic: Voices must appear in the main answer
user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Listing voices in Follow-up is not enough. If the user asks to use voices or LLM council, the answer body must contain actual voice reasoning blocks that materially constrain the answer.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Put voice blocks in Main Answer before Results and Follow-up. Follow-up remains proposed next prompts.

user_feedback_to_preserve.topic: LLM Council must be visible and separate
user_feedback_to_preserve.content: LLM Council is not just another voice and not just a follow-up option. It is a visible deliberation/audit layer that compares outputs, preserves dissent, rejects unsupported claims, and decides what survives.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Include a distinct LLM Council block in Main Answer when plurality/council is requested.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Add a stable Pushback voice with boxing glove emoji. Pushback challenges weak assumptions, soft boundaries, over-automation, fake progress, and unnecessary ceremony without being contrarian for its own sake.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Use 🥊 Pushback as a stable voice. It must name the weak assumption, consequence, and better boundary.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Do not change assigned voice emojis. Preserve Hume as 🦉, Zhuangzi as 🦋, Popper as 🧨, Feynman as 🔬, Orwell as ✒️, Factory as 🏭, Strategy as ♟️, Systems as 🔁, Audit as 🔍, Direct as 🎯, Wildcard as 🃏, and Pushback as 🥊.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Do not reassign or omit stable emojis when showing voice or lane labels.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Do not double-space between follow-up voice options. Preserve the full grouped field when tuning/plurality/follow-up behavior is active: Voices, Lanes, All compositions.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Use compact single-spaced follow-up lines. Do not flatten the grouped menu.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: All-of-the-above and All composition entries must use full operational language, not just names. They should be compact but must include composition, first packet, reason, spawn/defer/block truth, deliverable, and retreat condition.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Keep All-A/B/C/D and option 18 operational, compact, and not reduced to a label.

user_feedback_to_preserve.topic: Follow-ups are proposed prompts unless explicitly run
user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Follow-up entries are potential next prompts. If pre-run or simulated, say so. Do not imply they already executed unless worker receipts exist.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Results report what ran; Follow-up proposes what can run next.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: When the user asks for max waves/subagents or all voices, run real bounded workers up to runtime capacity, then continue in waves. Report spawned, blocked, deferred, and shutdown truth.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Close completed workers, spawn real lanes, preserve worker IDs/receipts, and do not fake plurality.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: Reduce user cognitive load by preserving capability while compressing prose. Do not make the user track internal policy conflicts unless they change the outcome.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: Answer first, surface only active constraints, keep receipts compact, expand full machinery only for tuning/plurality/deep audit turns.

user_feedback_to_preserve.content: User feedback is part of the work product and should be saved into Codex memory/process docs so repeated corrections are not forgotten.
user_feedback_to_preserve.operational_effect: When the user identifies repeated forgotten feedback, create or update JSON memory packets under /Users/joshuaeisenhart/wiki/codex-memory.

default_order: Main Answer with actual voices/council when requested
response_shape_rule.hard_boundary: Results != Follow-up != Voice Blocks != LLM Council
response_shape_rule.tiny_feedback_behavior: Apply the correction and continue the prior task; do not turn minor feedback into the whole answer.
